deep_dive.title: Machine Learning for Depression Screening and Intervention: an Original Circadian Rhythm Score-based Methodology
deep_dive.abstract: Depression screening from large-scale behavioral data is challenged by fragmented circadian indicators, limited interpretability, and the lack of intervention-oriented analysis. Existing approaches typically analyze sleep, activity, and social behaviors in isolation, failing to capture their joint circadian structure. To address this limitation, we first propose the Circadian Rhythm Score (CRS), a composite index that compresses multi-domain daily behaviors into a unified representation of circadian rhythm. CRS is constructed to maximize discriminative power for depression screening while preserving behavioral semantics through non-negativity constraints. Empirical results demonstrate near-lossless compression, where a single CRS retains almost the full predictive capability compared with multiple raw behavioral indicators. Building upon CRS, we develop an interpretable depression screening framework based on gradient-boosted trees and SHAP analysis, revealing nonlinear and saturation-like associations between circadian rhythm and depression risk. Beyond risk prediction, we further integrate interaction modeling and counterfactual regression to estimate heterogeneous and dose-dependent behavioral effects, enabling intervention-oriented reasoning under different circadian contexts. Experiments on the China Health and Retirement Longitudinal Study (CHARLS, n=15,233), demonstrate robust screening performance (ROC-AUC=0.825) and identify actionable behavioral thresholds, including a minimum effective exercise dose of approximately 300 MET-min/week and an optimal restorative nap duration of approximately 65 minutes for sleep-deprived individuals. By bridging supervised representation learning and interpretable modeling, this work provides a scalable framework for depression screening and intervention-aware healthcare data mining.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: digital phenotyping for depression using behavioral data, analogous to EMA-based monitoring
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用行为数据进行抑郁的数字表型，类似基于EMA的监测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用行为数据进行抑郁的数字表型，类似基于EMA的监测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用行为数据进行抑郁的数字表型，类似基于EMA的监测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Circadian Rhythm Score (CRS) compressing multi-domain daily behaviors into a unified representation for depression screening, achieving near-lossless compression and interpretability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 抑郁筛查面临昼夜节律指标碎片化、可解释性差等问题。本文提出昼夜节律评分(CRS)，通过非负约束将多域日常行为（睡眠、活动、社交）压缩为统一表征，最大化抑郁筛查判别力，同时保留行为语义。实验表明CRS实现了近乎无损的压缩，为数字表型和抑郁监测提供了可解释的综合性指标。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 抑郁筛查面临昼夜节律指标碎片化、可解释性差等问题。本文提出昼夜节律评分(CRS)，通过非负约束将多域日常行为（睡眠、活动、社交）压缩为统一表征，最大化抑郁筛查判别力，同时保留行为语义。实验表明CRS实现了近乎无损的压缩，为数字表型和抑郁监测提供了可解释的综合性指标。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: digital phenotyping using EMA in mental health
